$comment: End-to-end test fixture: real zustand scan data, extracted from docs/GitHub-Scanner-zustand.md (scanned 2026-04-16 against commit 32013285). Used by tests/test_end_to_end.py.

repo_metadata.name: zustand
repo_metadata.description: Small, fast, unopinionated state-management library for React.

Guards against accidental npm publish from a contributor machine

entries.title: Governance single-point-of-failure: no branch protection, no rulesets, no CODEOWNERS on a 57k-star npm library (C20)
entries.action_hint: Enable branch protection on main (require PR review + status checks) and add .github/CODEOWNERS covering .github/workflows and src/.
entries.boundary_note: 31 days since latest release v5.0.12 — 1 day outside the 30-day Critical window
entries.how_to_fix: GitHub *Settings → Branches → Add branch protection rule* ([direct link](https://github.com/pmndrs/zustand/settings/branch_protection_rules/new)). For `main`: require pull request reviews (1 approver minimum), require status checks (the existing `test.yml` matrix), require branches to be up to date before merging, do not allow bypassing. Also add `.github/CODEOWNERS` covering at minimum `.github/workflows/ @dai-shi @drcmda` and `src/ @dai-shi @drcmda`.

entries.title: Dependabot vulnerability alerts are disabled on a 57k-star public repo (version updates still flow)
entries.action_hint: Flip Dependabot alerts on in Settings → Code security (version updates are already running; this just re-enables the alert surface).
entries.how_to_fix: [github.com/pmndrs/zustand/settings/security_analysis](https://github.com/pmndrs/zustand/settings/security_analysis) → toggle *Dependabot alerts* to on. Takes five seconds and does not change any workflow or commit behaviour — it just turns the alert surface back on.

meta_table.label: Runtime deps
meta_table.value: ✅ Zero — blast radius limited to devDeps

entries.title: Clean code-path — zero runtime deps, zero install hooks, zero dangerous primitives
entries.action_hint: No action needed. Positive signal: the library does what a library should do and nothing more.

entries.title: Publish pipeline uses OIDC and `minimumReleaseAge` defensive config
entries.action_hint: No action needed. Maintainers are using the current-best-practice npm publish path.

entries.title: Maintainers and CI hygiene are solid
entries.action_hint: No action needed. Positive signal: the people who have the credentials are the kind you want to have them.

entries.title: OSSF Token-Permissions scored 0/10: 4 of 8 CI workflows lack explicit `permissions:` blocks
entries.action_hint: Add a top-level permissions: {} (deny all) to each of the 4 workflows, then grant only the scopes each job needs.
entries.how_to_fix: Add a top-level `permissions: {}` (empty — deny all) to each of the 4 workflows, then grant only the specific scopes each job needs. For test-only workflows, `permissions: { contents: read }` is usually sufficient. See [GitHub docs on workflow permissions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-jobs/assigning-permissions-to-jobs).

meta_table.label: Practical risk
meta_table.value: ℹ Low (public repo, default scope is minimal)

entries.action: Install is safe. Pin exact versions + set minimumReleaseAge: 1440 in your pnpm config for production. Nudge maintainers on branch protection + Dependabot alerts.
Nothing you need to do locally. For maintainers: enable branch protection on main and re-enable Dependabot alerts.

split_axis_decision.reasoning: The library itself is identically clean for every consumer, but the consumer-side mitigation (minimumReleaseAge + exact pinning) only matters in production/shared-host contexts. Solo-dev and production ship different action cards.

entries.headline: Install and use — the library itself is clean
entries.summary: Pure library, zero runtime deps, zero install-time hooks. Caveat: upstream governance gaps (no branch protection, no CODEOWNERS, Dependabot alerts off). Install today is safe; residual risk is supply-chain credential compromise affecting future releases.

entries.headline: Pair with minimumReleaseAge in your consumer config
entries.summary: Same verdict, stronger caveat. Because pmndrs/zustand has no upstream governance gate, a compromised maintainer credential would ship arbitrary code to every install on the next release. Mitigation: minimumReleaseAge: 1440, pin exact versions in production package.json.

selections.reason: Falsification: if branch-protection endpoint returned 200 or rulesets were non-empty, the F0 verdict would move to Clean
Falsification: if Dependabot alerts API returned 200, F1 would close as a non-finding

paths: credential phishing against dai-shi or drcmda
stale OAuth token reuse from an old CI system
compromised browser session cookie via a malicious extension
malicious IDE/editor extension running as the maintainer
sloppy review of an attacker-submitted PR

entries.headline: Install zustand normally — the library is clean
If you ship zustand in production, pin exact versions and set minimumReleaseAge
Nudge the maintainers on the two governance gaps

catalog_metadata.short_description: Small, fast, unopinionated state-management library for React. Stores live in hooks, no provider wrapper required; middleware surface covers persistence, devtools bridge, Immer, and Redux-style APIs. Distributed as the zustand package on npm with zero runtime dependencies.

section_01.pill_row: ✓ Safe to install today · ⚠ 1 consumer-side mitigation · 3 steps
section_01.summary: **Install is safe** — the library itself is clean (Step 1). If you ship to production, pair zustand with `minimumReleaseAge` in your own pnpm config to buy time against an upstream credential-compromise scenario (Step 2). Step 3 is a maintainer-side ask you can pass along.

groups.headline: Governance gaps — no branch protection, no CODEOWNERS, Dependabot alerts off
groups.one_line_summary: C20 single-point-of-failure fires as Warning (release age 31 days, 1 day outside Critical window); alert surface disabled while version-update PRs still flow

items.claim: No branch protection on `main` (API returns 404), zero rulesets (`/rulesets` returns `[]`, `/rules/branches/main` returns `[]`), no CODEOWNERS in any of 4 standard locations. On a 57k-star library imported by millions, this is a supply-chain single-point-of-failure. Warning (not Critical) because the latest release v5.0.12 is 31 days old — 1 day outside the 30-day active-flow window that would escalate it.

The Dependabot alerts API returns 403 with the explicit "disabled for this repository" message — vulnerability alerts are turned off. Dependabot VERSION updates ARE still active (`dependabot[bot]` is the #6 contributor with 18 commits), so this is specifically the alert surface being disabled rather than a totally broken integration.

groups.headline: Strong positive signals — clean code-path, defensive supply chain, long-tenured maintainers
groups.one_line_summary: Zero runtime deps, zero install hooks, OIDC npm publish, pnpm `minimumReleaseAge`, SHA-pinned actions, no `pull_request_target`, active Copilot-bot hygiene PRs, pmndrs collective

items.claim: Zero runtime dependencies. Peer deps only (`react`, `@types/react`, `immer`, `use-sync-external-store`). The inbound supply chain at install time is effectively "zustand itself" — minimal attack surface for transitive CVEs.

No `preinstall`, `postinstall`, `prepare`, `prepublish`, `prepack`, or `postpack` scripts in `package.json`. `"private": true` guards against accidental `npm publish`; release is gated through CI only.
`publish.yml` uses OIDC `id-token: write` to authenticate to npm — no long-lived npm token stored in GitHub Secrets. Published from `dist/` on `release: published` event only.
`pnpm-workspace.yaml` contains `minimumReleaseAge: 1440` — blocks installs of dep versions less than 24 hours old. Zustand uses it on itself.
7 of 8 external actions across the 8 workflows are SHA-pinned to commit hashes. Zero `pull_request_target` usage.
Recent Copilot-bot PR #3395: "Pin `actions/deploy-pages` to commit SHA for supply-chain security" — ongoing pinning improvement landed in the last scan window.

Grep scan of `src/` found zero `eval(`, zero `new Function(`, zero `child_process`/`execSync`/`spawnSync`, zero `fetch(`/`axios`/`http.get`. The single `.exec()` hit in `src/middleware/devtools.ts:177` is a regex-match, not a process-exec.

dai-shi (15-year GitHub account, 8,088 followers, 411 commits) and drcmda (pmndrs founder, 13-year account, 4,215 followers, 232 commits). All top contributors are real people with long tenure — no sockpuppet signals.

executable_file_inventory.lead: ✓ 0 install-time executables · ℹ 13 runtime files inventoried. **Zustand ships zero install-time executables** — it's a pure library with no CLIs, no binaries, no lifecycle scripts. No file in this inventory has an actionable dangerous-primitive hit; a single regex `.exec()` call in `devtools.ts:177` is not a process-exec.
executable_file_inventory.layer1_summary: ✓ **Zustand ships 0 install-time executables — pure library.** No CLIs, no binaries, no `pre/post/prepare` hooks. Runtime code is 16 TypeScript source files (7 top-level modules + 7 middleware + shallow helpers + types) compiled to `dist/` and published as the `zustand` npm package.

layer2_entries.notes: May touch `localStorage`/`sessionStorage` via user-provided storage adapter
Posts messages to `window.__REDUX_DEVTOOLS_EXTENSION__`. The `.exec()` at line 177 is `RegExp.prototype.exec`
Shims dispatch/reducer onto a zustand store

executable_file_inventory.closing_note: **Note on the one `.exec()` hit.** The Step A dangerous-primitive grep surfaced exactly one `.exec()` call across `src/`: `src/middleware/devtools.ts:177`. This is not a process-exec. It's `RegExp.prototype.exec()` called on the caller-line string that DevTools uses to label the action in its timeline UI. No `child_process`, no `spawn`, no `execFile`, no shell.

pr_sample_review.intro: **Recent PR activity is routine.** Sample of 7 most recent merges shows README fixes, Dependabot devDep bumps, docs-link updates, and a persist-hydration bugfix. One self-merge in the sample (dai-shi merged his own devDep bump) — common for a maintainer-owned library, low risk because the PR was a routine dep version bump.

rows.what: Update TypeScript guide links in README
rows.author: FelixEckl (CONTRIBUTOR)
rows.concern: Docs only. Different author/merger — practical pair-of-eyes.

rows.author: Copilot (bot)
rows.concern: **Positive signal** — bot proposed pinning, maintainer merged.

phase_4_structured_llm.timeline_intro: Six beats across the library's history. **The story is steady ship-and-fix** — 100+ releases over 7 years, monthly cadence in recent months, ongoing hygiene work, no security incidents, no yanked versions. One process note: the latest release shipped 31 days ago, which puts the C20 governance finding on the Warning side of the boundary but would flip it to Critical on the next scan unless governance is added first.

rows.note: Created 2019-04-09 — mature library with established patterns
15-year GitHub account, 127 public repos, 8,088 followers — no sockpuppet signals
pmndrs founder, 13-year account, 95 public repos, 4,215 followers
~29% formal review. Cross-merging provides practical second-pair-of-eyes. OSSF Code-Review: 6/10.
API 404 + empty rulesets + empty rules/branches/main — authoritative
Checked 4 standard locations — no file in any
Alerts API 403 with "disabled for this repository" message. Version updates still active.
Never an advisory-class incident in 7 years. OSSF Security-Policy: 0/10 (no SECURITY.md).
No long-lived NPM_TOKEN stored in GitHub Secrets
pnpm defensive config set in repo's own `pnpm-workspace.yaml`
Small library; tarball extraction surfaced 143 files / 2.2M uncompressed
Independent security rating from the [Open Source Security Foundation](https://securityscorecards.dev/). Scores 24 practices 0-10; most repos 3-5, above 6 is strong.

phase_4_structured_llm.coverage_intro: **All 12 C11 coverage cells verified.** 8/8 workflow files read, full tarball extracted (143 files, 2.2M), 0 `pull_request_target`, 0 agent-rule files, 0 prompt-injection hits, 0 README paste-blocks, no Windows surface, not a true monorepo. One caveat: 7 of 8 external actions are SHA-pinned; the one remaining reference (`pmndrs/docs/.github/workflows/build.yml@v3`) is a same-org reusable workflow rather than a third-party action.

rows.result: ✅ OK — 143 files, 2.2M — pinned to `32013285083648e8d58ba1f76d73b9bdc02fef50` at scan start
✅ 8 of 8 — test, publish, preview-release, compressed-size, docs, test-multiple-builds, test-multiple-versions, test-old-typescript
✅ Verified — `pnpm-workspace.yaml` lists `packages: [- .]`, not a true monorepo
✅ Verified — 0 paste-blocks. Single fenced code block is `npm install zustand`
✅ Verified — 0 matches / 0 actionable. Scanner Integrity section NOT emitted
⚠ Install path: 1/1 · Artifact: 0/1. Single npm channel. README matches CI. Artifact verification would require running the pipeline end-to-end and sha256-matching the published tarball.
✅ Verified — no Windows surface (no .ps1/.bat/.cmd anywhere). Pure library.
✅ Verified — 0 actionable hits. Single `.exec()` is regex-match. Positive control: 22 `import` statements found.
⚠ 7 of 64 recent merges sampled — 2/7 formal (29%), 6/7 cross-merger (86%), 1/7 self-merge (14%)

pr_target_usage.note: Zero `pull_request_target` usage — rules out that class of attack

entries: Artifact reproducibility not verified — end-artifact was not independently rebuilt from source and sha-diffed against the published npm tarball.

Dependabot ALERTS are disabled (confirmed via explicit 403 message) — but Dependabot VERSION UPDATES are active. So we know the alert surface is off, but we did not separately enumerate the current dep-graph for unpatched CVEs.

Review-rate metric is based on a 7-PR sample, not the full 300-PR window. The pattern (formal review ~29%, practical cross-merge ~100%) is consistent across the sample but a larger enumeration would tighten the estimate.

phase_4_structured_llm.evidence_intro: 9 command-backed claims. **Skip ahead to items marked ★ START HERE** — the branch-protection-missing API check and the Dependabot-alerts-disabled diagnostic. Those two are the falsification criteria for the Warning verdict: if either reversed, the verdict would move to Clean.

entries.claim: No branch protection, no rulesets, no CODEOWNERS on `main` (F0 / C20)
entries.command:
gh api "repos/pmndrs/zustand/branches/main/protection"
gh api "repos/pmndrs/zustand/rulesets"
gh api "repos/pmndrs/zustand/rules/branches/main"
for p in CODEOWNERS .github/CODEOWNERS docs/CODEOWNERS .gitlab/CODEOWNERS; do
  gh api "repos/pmndrs/zustand/contents/$p" 2>&1 | head -1
done
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — all four governance signals are negative. 404 on branch protection is authoritative (scan token has `repo` scope; permission-denied would be 403). Empty rulesets and rules/branches/main together capture both repo-level and org-level rulesets. CODEOWNERS absent in all four standard locations. Warning-level because release age (31 days) is 1 day outside the 30-day Critical window.

entries.claim: Dependabot vulnerability alerts are disabled (version updates still active)
entries.command:
gh api "repos/pmndrs/zustand/dependabot/alerts" 2>&1 | head -3
gh api "repos/pmndrs/zustand/contributors?per_page=10" -q '.[] | select(.login=="dependabot[bot]")'
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — the 403 message is diagnostic ("disabled for this repository"), distinguishing a setting-level disable from a scan-token-scope issue. Separately, `dependabot[bot]` appears as the #6 contributor with 18 commits, so Dependabot VERSION UPDATES are active.

entries.claim: Review-rate sample on 7 recent merges — 2 formal, 7 any-contact
entries.command:
gh pr list -R pmndrs/zustand --state merged --limit 7 \
  --json number,title,author,mergedBy,reviewDecision,reviews \
  | jq '.[] | {pr: .number, author: .author.login, merger: .mergedBy.login,
               formal: .reviewDecision, any_review: ((.reviews|length) > 0)}'
entries.result:
PR#3466: author=FelixEckl, merger=dai-shi, formal=null, any=false
PR#3447: author=dependabot[bot], merger=dbritto-dev, formal=null, any=false
PR#3443: author=mahmoodhamdi, merger=dbritto-dev, formal=null, any=false
PR#3442: author=mahmoodhamdi, merger=dbritto-dev, formal=null, any=false
PR#3427: author=dai-shi, merger=dai-shi, formal=null, any=false  [SELF-MERGE]
PR#3423: author=pavan-sh, merger=dai-shi, formal=APPROVED, any=true
PR#3414: author=grigoriy-reshetniak, merger=dai-shi, formal=APPROVED, any=true
Totals: 2/7 formal (29%), 6/7 cross-merger (86%), 1/7 self-merge (14%)
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — 2 of 7 have `reviewDecision = APPROVED`. 6 of 7 have different author/merger. The one self-merge is a dai-shi dev-deps bump (MEMBER association).
Confirmed fact — empty array. None of the six lifecycle-hook script names are present in `package.json`. Combined with `"private": true`, release is gated through CI only.

entries.claim: OIDC token used for npm publish
entries.result:
permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read
# (triggered on release: published only; publishes from ./dist)
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — `id-token: write` grants the workflow an OIDC token that npm exchanges for a short-lived publish credential. No long-lived `NPM_TOKEN` is stored in GitHub Secrets.

entries.claim: Dangerous-primitive grep — single `.exec()` is a regex match, not process-exec
entries.command:
grep -rnE '\beval\(|\bnew Function\(|child_process|execSync|spawnSync' \
  /tmp/scan-zustand/zustand-src/src/
grep -rn '\.exec(' /tmp/scan-zustand/zustand-src/src/
grep -rn '^import ' /tmp/scan-zustand/zustand-src/src/ | wc -l   # positive control
entries.result:
(no matches for eval/new Function/child_process/execSync/spawnSync)
src/middleware/devtools.ts:177:  const match = callerLineRegex.exec(callerLine)
22   # positive control — grep tool working
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — the only `.exec()` hit is `RegExp.prototype.exec()` against a caller-line string, used for DevTools action-name labelling. Not a process-exec. The 22-import positive control confirms the grep infrastructure was working.
Confirmed fact — 15-year and 13-year GitHub accounts, large followings, substantial public repo counts. No sockpuppet signals.
Confirmed fact — 1440 minutes = 24 hours. pnpm will refuse to install a dep version younger than 24 hours. The same defensive config the Step 2 action-card recommends to consumers.

Confirmed fact — 8 workflow files, zero `pull_request_target` occurrences. That event type is the vector in most GitHub Actions PwnRequest supply-chain incidents; its absence here rules out that class of attack.

does_anyone_check_the_code.short_answer: Review happens but isn't required — ~29% formal review rate in sample, no branch protection, no CODEOWNERS

do_they_fix_problems_quickly.short_answer: Yes — long-tenured multi-maintainer pmndrs collective; issues triaged promptly

do_they_tell_you_about_problems.short_answer: Yes — monthly releases, active Copilot-bot hygiene PRs, CHANGELOG maintained

is_it_safe_out_of_the_box.short_answer: Yes — clean code-path, no install hooks, no network calls, zero runtime deps

editorial_caption.text: A 57,000-star React state library with zero runtime dependencies, no install-time hooks, and a clean code surface — but no branch protection, no CODEOWNERS, and Dependabot alerts disabled on one of the most-imported libraries on npm.

entries.what_we_observed: Classic branch protection on main returns 404. Rulesets: []. Rules on main: []. CODEOWNERS absent in all four standard locations.
entries.what_this_means: When you install zustand, you are trusting the maintainers' account security for a package imported by millions of projects. There is no automated gate that would catch a phished-credential push before it reached your next install.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: The library itself is clean — zero runtime deps, zero install hooks, no network, no dangerous primitives. This finding is purely about the upstream process gap, not about current code shipping something harmful.

entries.what_we_observed: Dependabot alerts API returns 403 with the explicit 'disabled for this repository' message. Dependabot VERSION updates ARE still active (dependabot[bot] #6 contributor, 18 commits).
entries.what_this_means: On a repo imported by millions of consumers, turning off the alert surface is a hygiene gap. GHSA-class CVEs published against devDeps would go unsurfaced to the maintainers.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: It does not mean the repo is unmaintained. Active Copilot-bot PRs, OIDC publish, and minimumReleaseAge all indicate deliberate supply-chain hygiene. This specific toggle looks like an oversight.

entries.what_we_observed: OSSF Scorecard Token-Permissions check scored 0/10. 4 of 8 workflows lack a top-level permissions: block. publish.yml correctly restricts to id-token: write + contents: read.
entries.what_this_means: Explicit permissions: blocks are a defense-in-depth best practice. OSSF scores the absence heavily.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: This does not mean the workflows are insecure. The publish.yml (the only workflow that matters for npm releases) already has correct, minimal permissions with OIDC.

does_anyone_check_the_code.short_answer: Review happens but isn't required — ~29% formal review rate in sample, no branch protection, no CODEOWNERS

do_they_fix_problems_quickly.short_answer: Yes — long-tenured multi-maintainer pmndrs collective; issues triaged promptly

do_they_tell_you_about_problems.short_answer: Yes — monthly releases, active Copilot-bot hygiene PRs, CHANGELOG maintained

is_it_safe_out_of_the_box.short_answer: Yes — clean code-path, no install hooks, no network calls, zero runtime deps
